name: Mozar - tour around the Castron and Tera rivers
name_original: Mózar – vuelta por los ríos Castrón y Tera
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Villanázar are situated within the tranquil rural landscape of Zamora province, Spain. The region is characterized by quiet country roads, extensive fields, and the presence of river valleys, including the Tera and Esla rivers. Terrain typically features rolling hills, offering varied gradients suitable for road cyclists. This area provides a base for exploring the broader network of routes across Zamora, known for its distinct Spanish countryside. Best road cycling routes around Villanázar The most popular road cycling route is Mozar - tour around Benavente and Bretó - the rivers Esla and Tera, a 28.8 miles (46.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 52 minutes to complete. This easy route follows the courses of the Esla and Tera rivers near Benavente. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Vidriales Valley Roman Sites Loop, a moderate 33.7 miles (54.2 km) path. This route explores the Vidriales Valley, passing by various Roman sites. Local road cyclists also love the Mozar - tour around the Castron and Tera rivers, a 42.2 miles (68.0 km) trail leading through river valleys, often completed in about 2 hours 40 minutes. Road cycling around Villanázar is defined by rural countryside, rolling hills, and river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside paths to more challenging routes with sustained climbs. The routes in Villanázar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from around 10 reviews. More than 200 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Villanázar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Villanázar?
A: There are over 35 road cycling routes available around Villanázar, offering a variety of options for different skill levels. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes near Villanázar?
A: The region around Villanázar is characterized by quiet country roads, extensive fields, and river valleys, including the Tera and Esla rivers. You can expect mostly rolling hills, providing varied gradients suitable for road cyclists. The wider Zamora province also offers diverse landscapes, from the dramatic canyons of Arribes del Duero to the tranquil banks of the Duero River.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Villanázar?
A: Yes, Villanázar offers several easy road cycling routes. An excellent option for beginners is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1024514543/mozar-tour-around-benavente-and-breto-the-rivers-esla-and-tera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mozar - tour around Benavente and Bretó - the rivers Esla and Tera&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 28.8-mile (46.3 km) trail that follows the courses of the Esla and Tera rivers.

Q: Can I find challenging road cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: While most routes are moderate, there are options for more experienced cyclists seeking longer distances and varied climbs. The region's rolling hills provide engaging terrain. For a moderate challenge, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1023238917/vidriales-valley-roman-sites-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vidriales Valley Roman Sites Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 33.7-mile (54.2 km) path that explores historical sites.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes around Villanázar?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1023238917/vidriales-valley-roman-sites-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vidriales Valley Roman Sites Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular example, offering a moderate ride through scenic and historical areas.

Q: What landmarks or points of interest can I see along road cycling routes near Villanázar?
A: The routes often pass by interesting historical and natural landmarks. You might encounter various bridges, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5422592" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridge of the Vía Verde de la Plata&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2810181" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridge over the Esla River&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1023238917/vidriales-valley-roman-sites-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vidriales Valley Roman Sites Loop&lt;/a&gt; specifically explores Roman sites. The wider Zamora province is also rich in Romanesque art and historic towns like Benavente, which features the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5151970" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Santa María del Azogue&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there historical sites to explore while cycling in the region?
A: Absolutely. The Zamora province, where Villanázar is located, is known for its rich historical heritage. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1023238917/vidriales-valley-roman-sites-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vidriales Valley Roman Sites Loop&lt;/a&gt; take you past ancient Roman sites. Additionally, the Vía de la Plata, an ancient route and part of the Camino de Santiago, traverses the province, offering a historic path for cyclists. Many towns in the region, including &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2720898" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Benavente&lt;/a&gt;, boast traditional architecture and Romanesque churches.

Q: Is parking available for road cyclists in Villanázar?
A: Villanázar is a tranquil rural municipality, and while specific designated cycling parking areas are not widely advertised, you can typically find parking in the village itself. It's advisable to check local regulations or inquire upon arrival, especially if you plan to start a longer route from the village center.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Villanázar?
A: The best time for road cycling in Villanázar and the wider Zamora province is generally during the spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October). During these seasons, the weather is typically mild and pleasant, with less extreme heat than summer and fewer cold temperatures than winter, making for ideal cycling conditions.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling options near Villanázar?
A: Yes, the region offers several easy routes that are suitable for families. The quiet country roads and relatively flat sections along the river valleys provide a safe and enjoyable environment for family cycling. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1024514543/mozar-tour-around-benavente-and-breto-the-rivers-esla-and-tera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mozar - tour around Benavente and Bretó - the rivers Esla and Tera&lt;/a&gt; is an easy route that could be a good option for families.

Q: Can I find long-distance road cycling routes in the Villanázar area?
A: While Villanázar itself is a starting point for local routes, its location within Zamora province provides access to longer-distance cycling opportunities. The Vía de la Plata (Silver Route), an ancient path, traverses the province and offers extensive scenic sections suitable for long-distance road cycling. Routes along the Duero River also provide extended scenic rides.

Q: Are there amenities like cafes or pubs along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes pass through or near small villages and towns where you can find local cafes, bars, or restaurants to refuel and rest. While Villanázar is a small municipality, larger towns like Benavente, which is often part of cycling routes, offer more extensive amenities. It's always a good idea to plan your route and check for available services in advance.
tips: Mózar is a small town located at the confluence of the Tera and Órbigo rivers in the Benavente valley. Famous for its natural activities, there are several campsites near the river beaches for bathing and abundant places for trout fishing.
A small town next to the Tera river valley, fertile lands that were occupied from the Celts. There is a beautiful Roman villa in the village with a mosaic floor.
Bridge over the Tera river between Sitrama and Santibáñez de Tera. The river is a tributary of the Esla river, a natural park of special conservation.

waypoints.name: Mózar River Beach on the Tera River
